Canadian music video director and producer

**Julien Christian Lutz** (born October 31, 1975), known professionally as **Director X**, is a Canadian music video director. Having trained under [Hype Williams](/wiki/Hype_Williams "Hype Williams"), Lutz has been credited for directing over a hundred [music videos](/wiki/Music_video "Music video") spanning various genres since 1998, including [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"), [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(musician) "Drake (musician)"), [Future](/wiki/Future_(rapper) "Future (rapper)"), [John Mayer](/wiki/John_Mayer "John Mayer"), [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West"), [DJ Khaled](/wiki/DJ_Khaled "DJ Khaled"), [Usher](/wiki/Usher_(musician) "Usher (musician)"), [Ice Cube](/wiki/Ice_Cube "Ice Cube"), [Little Mix](/wiki/Little_Mix "Little Mix"), [Fabolous](/wiki/Fabolous "Fabolous"), [Fifth Harmony](/wiki/Fifth_Harmony "Fifth Harmony"), [Mystikal](/wiki/Mystikal "Mystikal") and [Akon](/wiki/Akon "Akon"). In film, he directed the crime thriller *[Superfly](/wiki/Superfly_(2018_film) "Superfly (2018 film)")* in 2018, which saw mixed reception. In television, he created the short-lived action drama *[Robyn Hood](/wiki/Robyn_Hood "Robyn Hood")* in 2023.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Director_X&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Lutz was born and raised in the [Greater Toronto Area](/wiki/Greater_Toronto_Area "Greater Toronto Area"), mostly in and around [Brampton](/wiki/Brampton "Brampton"), and is of mixed Afro-Trinidadian and Swiss descent.[[1]](#cite_note-Directorxfilms-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Director_X&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### Early work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Director_X&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early work")]

Lutz began his career working on several productions; he was a visual consultant on [Hype Williams](/wiki/Hype_Williams "Hype Williams")' 1998 film *[Belly](/wiki/Belly_(film) "Belly (film)")*. Lutz was slated to direct Disney's horror feature *Razorwire*, though the film never moved into production.[[2]](#cite_note-SuperiorPics.com-2)

### Music videos

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Director_X&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Music videos")]

Lutz worked on several projects and trained under Hype Williams.[[3]](#cite_note-littlexonline-3) Since the late 1990s, Lutz has directed dozens of music videos and been noted for his high-budget, visually distinctive music videos, including for [The Wanted](/wiki/The_Wanted "The Wanted"), [R. Kelly](/wiki/R._Kelly "R. Kelly"), [Aaliyah](/wiki/Aaliyah "Aaliyah"), [DMX](/wiki/DMX "DMX"), [Mystikal](/wiki/Mystikal "Mystikal"), [Usher](/wiki/Usher_(entertainer) "Usher (entertainer)"), [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West"), [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"), [Korn](/wiki/Korn "Korn"), [David Guetta](/wiki/David_Guetta "David Guetta"), [Nelly Furtado](/wiki/Nelly_Furtado "Nelly Furtado"), [Sean Paul](/wiki/Sean_Paul "Sean Paul"), [Justin Bieber](/wiki/Justin_Bieber "Justin Bieber"), [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(entertainer) "Drake (entertainer)"), [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj"), [T.I.](/wiki/T.I. "T.I."), [Little Mix](/wiki/Little_Mix "Little Mix"), [Rihanna](/wiki/Rihanna "Rihanna"), [Union J](/wiki/Union_J "Union J"), [Fifth Harmony](/wiki/Fifth_Harmony "Fifth Harmony"), [Miguel](/wiki/Miguel_(singer) "Miguel (singer)"), [One Direction](/wiki/One_Direction "One Direction"), [Zayn Malik](/wiki/Zayn_Malik "Zayn Malik") and [Iggy Azalea](/wiki/Iggy_Azalea "Iggy Azalea").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] In 2004, Lutz appeared in [Mya Baker's](/wiki/Mya_Baker "Mya Baker") documentary film *Silence: In Search of Black Female Sexuality in America*.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In 2015, Lutz founded his own production company, Popp Rok, in Toronto.[[5]](#cite_note-5) In 2017, he appeared on episode 7 of Cycle 23 of *[America's Next Top Model](/wiki/America%27s_Next_Top_Model_(cycle_23) "America's Next Top Model (cycle 23)")* ("X Marks The Spot") as the director for a Gypsy Sports-themed music video.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### Films

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Director_X&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Films")]

In addition to music video directing, Lutz has directed two feature films and one [television film](/wiki/Television_film "Television film"); he made his feature [directorial debut](/wiki/List_of_directorial_debuts "List of directorial debuts") in 2015 with *[Across the Line](/wiki/Across_the_Line_(2015_film) "Across the Line (2015 film)").* The film starred [Stephan James](/wiki/Stephan_James_(actor) "Stephan James (actor)") and the storyline was inspired by the 1989 [Cole Harbour District High School](/wiki/Cole_Harbour_District_High_School "Cole Harbour District High School") race riots.[[7]](#cite_note-HCH13Apr20162-7) *Across the Line* was set and shot in Nova Scotia. The first screening of *Across the Line* was 19 September 2015 at the [Atlantic Film Festival](/wiki/Atlantic_Film_Festival "Atlantic Film Festival") where it won the award for "Best Atlantic Feature".[[7]](#cite_note-HCH13Apr20162-7)[[8]](#cite_note-HCH20Sep2015-8)

In 2016, he directed the [teen drama](/wiki/Teen_drama "Teen drama") television film *[Center Stage: On Pointe](/wiki/Center_Stage:_On_Pointe "Center Stage: On Pointe")*. The film stars [Nicole Muñoz](/wiki/Nicole_Mu%C3%B1oz "Nicole Muñoz"), Barton Cowperthwaite, Maude Green, [Chloe Lukasiak](/wiki/Chloe_Lukasiak "Chloe Lukasiak"),[[9]](#cite_note-9) [Kenny Wormald](/wiki/Kenny_Wormald "Kenny Wormald") and [Peter Gallagher](/wiki/Peter_Gallagher "Peter Gallagher"). It premiered on [Lifetime](/wiki/Lifetime_(TV_network) "Lifetime (TV network)") on June 25, 2016.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) The official DVD was released on September 6, 2016.[[12]](#cite_note-12) *[Variety](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")* critic Sonia Saraiya noted of Lutz's direction, "...though it is frustrating that the film feels like 15-odd low-budget music videos strung together, the dance in those segments, and the way it's filmed, are some of the best parts of the movie."[[13]](#cite_note-13)

In 2018, Lutz directed *[Superfly](/wiki/Superfly_(2018_film) "Superfly (2018 film)")*, a remake of the 1972 [blaxploitation](/wiki/Blaxploitation "Blaxploitation") [film of the same name.](/wiki/Super_Fly_(1972_film) "Super Fly (1972 film)")[[14]](#cite_note-14) *Superfly* was released in the United States on June 13, 2018, and received mixed reviews from critics. *Variety*'s [Owen Gleiberman](/wiki/Owen_Gleiberman "Owen Gleiberman") wrote: "Shot in a functional, slammed-together manner that's less sensually stylish than you'd expect from a music-video auteur, the film is a competent yet glossy and hermetic street-hustle drug thriller, less a new urban myth than a lavishly concocted episode. It holds your attention yet leaves you with nothing."[[15]](#cite_note-15)
